title: 10 best herbal creams for neuritis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with neuritis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds derived from the plant's leaves. These extracts work to reduce swelling and ease pain in affected nerves, providing relief from symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations. Additionally, the creams' antioxidant properties help protect damaged nerve tissue, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of further damage. As a result, maidenhair tree creams have become a popular natural remedy for managing neuritis and improving overall nerve health.
remedy_properties: Ginkgo biloba: Ginkgo biloba is believed to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which may help alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and pain.
St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum): St. John's Wort has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with neuritis.
Turmeric/Curcumin: Turmeric contains curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with neuritis.
Barks: The barks contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents that help soothe nerve damage and relieve symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils by submerging them in boiling water for 15 minutes.
Mix 200g of organic coconut oil with 100g of beeswax in a double boiler over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Add 20ml of ginkgo biloba extract (24% flavonoids) to the mixture and stir well for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 60 minutes then whip until smooth and creamy.
Fill the sterilized jars with the ginkgo biloba cream, leaving a 1cm headspace, and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with neuritis because of their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in damaged nerves. This makes it an effective treatment for conditions such as peripheral neuropathy, where nerve damage can cause chronic pain, numbness, and tingling. By reducing inflammation and alleviating pain, St John's Wort creams provide relief from the discomfort associated with neuritis, allowing individuals to regain mobility and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nerves, which can contribute to neuritis symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and burning pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected nerves, providing relief from neuritis symptoms like pain, stiffness, and limited mobility.

Pain-relieving properties: St John's Wort has been shown to have analgesic and anxiolytic effects, which can help alleviate chronic pain associated with neuritis, such as sciatica or shingles-related pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort contain hypericin, a flavonoid that exhibits analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to alleviate nerve pain and discomfort caused by neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and stems in early summer morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or desiccation methods within 24 hours for maximum preservation.
Grind dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Mix ground hypericum perforatum with coconut oil in a ratio of 1:5 by weight for optimal bioavailability and shelf life.
Heat the mixture under gentle stirring at 60-70°c for 30 minutes to facilitate absorption and emulsification.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with neuritis because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets inflammation in the nerves. Neuritis is characterized by inflammation of the nerve endings, which can cause numbness, tingling, and pain. Curcumin's ability to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress helps alleviate these symptoms, promoting healing and relief from discomfort. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties protect against further damage to the nerves, allowing them to function normally once again.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric creams containing curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with neuritis.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of turmeric creams can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of neuritis.
Pain-relieving property: Curcumin in turmeric creams has been shown to have analgesic effects, reducing pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
Roots: Contain a high amount of essential oils and resin, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh turmeric roots and clean them thoroughly with filtered water.
Dry the turmeric roots in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 4 hours.
Grind the dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 20g of the turmeric powder with 10g of coconut oil and 5g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 30 minutes until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng creams helps with neuritis because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate blood flow to affected nerves, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The cream's soothing properties also help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with neuritis. Additionally, ginseng's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects help protect damaged nerve tissue from further damage, allowing the body to naturally regenerate and repair itself. This natural approach can provide effective relief for those suffering from neuritis without the need for medication or surgery.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginseng cream's antioxidant properties help protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in cases of neuritis.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Ginseng cream's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain associated with neuritis, promoting relaxation and alleviating discomfort.
Neuroprotective activity: Ginseng cream's neuroprotective properties help protect and repair damaged nerve cells, reducing the risk of permanent damage or degeneration in cases of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, ginseng roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in alleviating neuritic pain.
Rhyzomes: Containing compounds like polysaccharides and glycosides, the rhyzomes of ginseng help to reduce inflammation and promote nerve regeneration, making them beneficial for treating neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean 50g of dried panax ginseng root, ensuring free from impurities and excess moisture.
Combine the dried panax ginseng root with 20g of sweet almond oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler, heating gently over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Add 5g of vitamin e oil and 5g of aloe vera gel to the mixture, stirring thoroughly and continuing to heat for an additional 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before whipping in 2% of triethanolamine and 1% of sodium hyaluronate, allowing the cream to thicken for 10 minutes.
Pour the final cream into sterilized containers, labeling and dating them while storing at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerated for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, penetrate deep into the skin to calm irritated nerves, reducing inflammation and pain associated with neuritis. Additionally, lavender's calming effect on the nervous system helps to reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate neuritic symptoms. As a result, English lavender creams provide effective relief from neuritic discomfort, promoting relaxation and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in damaged nerves, promoting healing and reducing pain associated with neuritis.

Sedative and analgesic effects: The calming and sedative properties of lavender cream can help alleviate nerve pain, numbness, or tingling sensations caused by neuritis, allowing for a more comfortable and restful sleep.

Antispasmodic and muscle relaxant properties: Lavender's antispasmodic compounds can help relax tense muscles and reduce spasms associated with neuritis, which can contribute to pain relief and improved mobility.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving neuritic pain.

Buds: Lavender buds are employed because they contain a higher amount of essential oils, particularly linalool, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of high-quality beeswax and 100g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup for melting.
Infuse 50g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 4-6 weeks.
Combine the melted beeswax with 300ml of distilled water and stir until well combined, then slowly add 100ml of the infused lavandula angustifolia oil.
Heat the mixture to a temperature of 180°f and maintain it at that level for 20 minutes while stirring occasionally, allowing the cream to thicken.

Remove the double boiler from heat, let it cool slightly, then strain the cream through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding any solids and preserving the cream in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with neuritis because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated nerves. The natural compounds found in valerian root, such as isovaleric acid and valerin, have a profound impact on the nervous system, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. By applying topical valerian cream to affected areas, individuals can experience a significant reduction in symptoms like numbness, tingling, and pain associated with neuritis, allowing for improved overall nerve function and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties help to reduce nerve inflammation and calm irritated nerves, alleviating symptoms of neuritis.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions: Valerian's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the nervous tissue, promoting healing and reducing pain associated with neuritis.
Pain-relieving and anesthetic properties: Valerian's anesthetic compounds help to numb affected areas, relieving chronic pain and discomfort caused by neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing nerve pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of valerian have been traditionally used to treat neuralgia and neuritis due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh valeriana officinalis roots and dry them thoroughly in shade for 7-10 days.
Grind dried valeriana officinalis roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine powdered valeriana officinalis with coconut oil at a ratio of 1:3 and stir well to form a smooth mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches 160°f.
Remove from heat and let cool completely before transferring to glass containers and storing in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with neuritis because of its unique combination of natural ingredients that target the root causes of the condition. The maypop plant, also known as Passiflora incarnata, contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected nerves. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can help to relax tense muscles and improve circulation, further reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Maypop cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and pain associated with neuritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.

Pain-relieving alkaloids: The alkaloids present in maypop cream, including berberine and chelerythrine, have analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties that help alleviate nerve pain and discomfort caused by neuritis.

Antioxidant activity: Maypop cream's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamins C and E, help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate neuritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate nerve pain and inflammation, making them a valuable ingredient for treating neuritis.
Barks: The barks contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in sufficient quantities for the recipe.
Combine one part of the herb mixture with three parts of sweet almond oil and warm gently over low heat.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 hours or overnight to allow the active compounds to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve for future use.
Whip one part of the infused oil with two parts of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat until fully incorporated and smooth to create the final cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw creams helps with neuritis because they contain the anti-inflammatory compounds harpagosides, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the nerves. These compounds work by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause swelling and discomfort. By reducing inflammation, devil's claw creams can provide relief from the numbness, tingling, and burning sensations associated with neuritis, allowing individuals to regain mobility and comfort in their affected limbs.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's claw contains anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagosides, which reduce swelling and pain in inflamed nerves, providing relief from neuritic symptoms.
Pain-relieving glycosides: The glycosides present in devil's claw, such as harpagoside and clematoside, have analgesic properties, helping to numb the pain associated with neuritis.
Antioxidant activity: Devil's claw's antioxidant properties help protect nerve tissues from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that can contribute to neuritic symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate inflammation and pain associated with neuritis.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing neuritic pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Collect 200g of dried harpagophytum procumbens root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered root with 100ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler and heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to separate the liquid from the solids and discard the solids after 15 minutes.
Mix the filtered oil with 10g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler and heat the mixture at 80°c for 20 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it into glass jars and storing it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow creams helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. The salicin present in white willow bark has a similar structure to aspirin, but works more gently on the body. When applied topically, it penetrates deeply into the skin and provides targeted relief for irritated nerves. This natural approach can offer long-term benefits by reducing inflammation and promoting healing, allowing sufferers of neuritis to find relief without the need for harsh chemicals or prescription medications.
remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin, a key active compound found in white willow bark, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in white willow cream have antioxidant properties, which help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing symptoms of neuritis.
Tannins: Tannins in white willow bark have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce swelling and prevent infections associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with neuritis.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and salicin, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for treating neuritis-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh willow bark extract by drying and grinding 10 grams of salix alba bark into a fine powder.
Infuse the willow bark powder in 100 milliliters of hot water at 90 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to create a concentrated decoction.
Combine the decoction with 200 grams of organic beeswax and 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil, heating the mixture until the wax melts at 65 degrees celsius.
Stir in 20 milliliters of liquid glycerin and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil, allowing the cream to cool and thicken for 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized containers and let it set at room temperature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's main active compound, calendula extract, penetrates deep into the skin to reduce swelling and ease pain in affected areas. Additionally, calendula's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties help to combat inflammation-causing free radicals and promote a healthy environment for nerve tissue repair. This natural approach helps alleviate discomfort and promote healing, making pot marigold creams a popular remedy for those suffering from neuritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with neuritis by reducing inflammation in the affected nerves.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in pot marigold cream help protect the nerves from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and regeneration.
Analgesic: Pot marigold's analgesic properties provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with neuritis, helping to soothe and calm irritated nerves.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce swelling and pain associated with neuritis.
Seeds: Abundant in fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds provide nourishment and protection to damaged nerves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven for at least eight hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground calendula powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heatproof glass measuring cup.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for one hour, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with neuritis because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol present in peppermint oil relaxes irritated nerves, reducing pain and discomfort associated with neuritis. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help reduce swelling and inflammation around the affected nerve, promoting faster healing. As a natural alternative to traditional treatments, herbal peppermint creams provide a soothing and gentle approach to alleviate the symptoms of neuritis, providing relief for those suffering from this condition.
remedy_properties: Pain Relief: The menthol and menthone compounds found in peppermint cream have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Peppermint's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected nerves, alleviating symptoms of neuritis.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidants present in peppermint cream, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which may contribute to neuritis development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other volatile oils, which help reduce inflammation and numb pain associated with neuritis.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate neuritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market in the early morning hours.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Grind 50g of dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of grated beeswax with 20g of coconut oil and 10g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Add the powdered peppermint to the melted wax-oil mixture and stir until well combined, allowing it to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory and calming properties. When applied topically, chamomile extracts soothe and calm irritated nerves, reducing inflammation and relieving pain associated with neuritis. The cream's gentle and non-greasy texture allows it to easily absorb into the skin, providing quick relief from discomfort. Additionally, chamomile's natural antispasmodic properties help relax muscles and reduce spasms, further alleviating symptoms of neuritis. Overall, chamomile creams offer a natural and effective solution for managing neuritis-related pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and pain in affected nerves, providing relief from neuritis symptoms.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic compounds relax muscle spasms and ease nerve tension, which can alleviate the sharp, shooting pains associated with neuritis.
Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's sedative properties calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which is often a companion symptom to neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated nerve tissues.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and pain associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in amounts of 20g for every 100ml of cream base mixture.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground matricaria chamomilla powder with distilled water at a ratio of 1:10 to form a paste, allowing it to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the paste through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused liquid.
Combine the infused liquid with a cream base mixture consisting of 100ml distilled water, 50ml cetyl alcohol, and 10g beeswax, heating the mixture gently until the ingredients are fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape creams helps with neuritis because of their unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory properties, and natural pain-relieving agents. The rich antioxidant profile in herbal grape creams has been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nerves, thereby alleviating symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations. Additionally, the cream's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds work together to soothe nerve pain, promoting faster healing and reducing discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Many herbs like turmeric, ginger, and arnica contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and pain associated with neuritis.

Pain-relieving properties: Herbs like willow bark, meadowsweet, and cayenne pepper contain salicins or capsaicin, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate neuralgia and pain in neuritis patients.

Antioxidant and neuroprotective properties: Certain herbs like ginkgo biloba, ashwagandha, and St. John's Wort contain antioxidants that can help protect damaged nerves and promote neuronal health, reducing the risk of further nerve damage or degeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help alleviate symptoms of neuritis by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry vitis vinifera leaves in a warm place for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried vitis vinifera leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered vitis vinifera leaves with two tablespoons of coconut oil in a small bowl.
Add three drops of peppermint essential oil to the mixture and stir well for five minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the affected nerves. The essential oil present in rosemary has been shown to increase circulation, improve oxygenation, and stimulate nerve function, helping to alleviate numbness, tingling, and burning sensations associated with neuritis. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a faster recovery from this painful condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Rosemary's antioxidant properties help protect the nerves from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to neuritis symptoms such as pain and inflammation.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in rosemary cream, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and alleviate inflammation in the affected nerves, providing relief from numbness, tingling, and discomfort associated with neuritis.

Neuroprotective Properties: Rosemary's neuroprotective properties help to protect the myelin sheath surrounding nerve fibers, which can be damaged in cases of neuritis, allowing for improved nerve function and reduced symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
Barks: Rosemary barks are rich in terpenes and volatile oils that help reduce inflammation and relieve nerve compression, providing relief from neuritic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of rosmarinus officinalis for sufficient quantity.
Dry the harvested leaves in shade at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Grind 20g of dried rosmarinus officinalis into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 20g of powdered rosmarinus officinalis with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Stir the mixture at room temperature for 2-3 hours until a homogenous cream forms.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which helps reduce swelling and pain associated with neuritis in the nerves.

Antioxidant properties: The cream's antioxidant properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve tissue, thereby alleviating symptoms of neuritis.

Pain-relieving properties: Kansas coneflower contains analgesic compounds like ursolic acid, which helps reduce pain and discomfort associated with neuritis by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.

Barks: The barks of the plant have been traditionally used to treat neuralgia, which is a type of pain or burning sensation that occurs along nerve pathways, making them suitable for medicinal creams for neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Gather echinacea angustifolia dried root extract of 10% concentration.
Combine the extracted powder with distilled water in a double boiler setup.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes at 180-200°f temperature and 15-20 pounds pressure.
Mix the cooled extract with beeswax and coconut oil in a ratio of 1:2:1 by weight.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify within 24 hours.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with neuritis because of their unique blend of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb, Echinacea purpurea, contains compounds that target inflammation in the nerves, reducing pain and discomfort associated with conditions like sciatica and peripheral neuritis. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties soothe and calm irritated nerve endings, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms. By addressing these underlying issues, Kansas coneflower creams provide effective relief from neuritic symptoms, allowing individuals to regain mobility and comfort in their daily lives.

Feverfew creams helps with neuritis because they contain parthenolide, a compound that reduces inflammation and relaxes muscles. When applied topically, feverfew cream can soothe irritated nerves and ease discomfort caused by conditions such as trigeminal neuralgia and sciatica. The anti-inflammatory properties of feverfew also help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, providing quick relief from symptoms of neuritis. Additionally, feverfew's natural antispasmodic effects can help to calm muscle spasms and cramping that often accompany neuritis.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's active compound parthenolide has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with neuritis.

Antioxidant activity: Feverfew's antioxidant properties can help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which is thought to contribute to the development of neuritis.

Spasmolytic activity: Feverfew has been shown to have spasmolytic properties, meaning it can help relax muscle spasms and reduce inflammation in the nerves, which can provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and relieve nerve pain associated with neuritis.
Flowers: The flowers of the feverfew plant contain parthenolide, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of tanacetum parthenium, measuring out one part by weight for every four parts of carrier oil.
Combine the dried flowers with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a ratio of 1:4 by weight.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place, shaking occasionally, to allow for active compounds extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil to the infused oil at a ratio of 10% by weight, stirring until fully incorporated and whipped.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with neuritis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in affected nerves. The creams' natural soothing properties calm irritated nerve endings, promoting healthy tissue regeneration and repair. Additionally, hops' neuroprotective agents help to shield nerves from further damage, hastening the recovery process. By targeting inflammation and promoting nerve health, herbal hops creams offer a safe and effective way to manage neuritis symptoms, providing relief for those suffering from this often debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal hops creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like humulones and lupulones, which help reduce swelling and pain in affected nerves, providing relief from neuritis symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in herbal hops creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing inflammation.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Herbal hops creams also contain anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative compounds like linalool and myrcene, which can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, which is often disrupted in individuals experiencing neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving neuralgia and neuritis pain.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, buds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves in early morning mist and dry them for 2-3 hours under shade.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered humulus lupulus with 20% beeswax, 30% coconut oil, and 50% sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30-40 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before transferring it to glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. The cream's ability to ease inflammation also diminishes pressure on the nerve endings, allowing them to function properly again. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing and calming effects help to calm irritated nerves, promoting relaxation and reducing tension that can exacerbate neuritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, which can alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as pain and numbness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and ease nerve compression, providing relief from neuritis-related discomfort.
Sedative and antispasmodic effects: Lemon balm's calming and relaxing properties can help to reduce muscle spasms and cramping associated with neuritis, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with neuritis.
Leaves: Contain rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that soothes nerve irritation and reduces swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or low-temperature dehydrators within 24 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm, dark place for 14 days, shaking daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter within 30 minutes of preparation time.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for neuritis are topical preparations containing natural ingredients that soothe and calm irritated nerves, providing relief from the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis. These creams work by reducing inflammation and promoting healing, allowing patients to enjoy a better quality of life. Examples include creams infused with arnica, turmeric, and ginger, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms. By using these herbal creams, individuals with neuritis can experience reduced pain, improved mobility, and increased confidence, enabling them to engage in daily activities without discomfort or limitation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat neuritis with herbal creams is to apply a combination of cooling and anti-inflammatory herbs directly to the affected area. Topical creams containing herbs like St. John's Wort, Arnica, and Witch Hazel help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, while soothing herbs like Lavender and Chamomile promote relaxation and calmness. Regular application of these creams can provide effective relief from neuritis symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neuritis that are treated with herbal creams are conditions such as neuralgia, sciatica, and rheumatoid arthritis. These herbal creams typically contain a combination of anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving herbs like arnica, devil's claw, and capsaicin, which help to reduce inflammation, ease muscle tension, and alleviate chronic pain.
